person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir Simon Fraser of Oliver and Neidpath was a Scottish knight who fought in the Wars of Scottish Independence, for which he was hanged, drawn, and quartered in 1306.

«b»Life«/b»
Simon Fraser was the son of Simon Fraser (died 1291) and Maria.

Fraser was captured during the Battle of Dunbar on 27 April 1296 and was sent to a prison in England. He was forfeited of his lands. He was released to serve King Edward I of England's expedition in Flanders in 1297. Fraser was made a household knight-effectively, a member of the royal bodyguard - and gifted a horse by Edward before the Battle of Falkirk. Fraser was thus among the English cavalry divisions that defeated the army of William Wallace. On 27 March 1299, in recognition of his good service, he was restored his lands and titles. He was the Keeper of Selkirk Forest, and was at the Siege of Carlaverock on the side of the English in 1300.

He switched to the Scottish side in mid-1301 and led the Scottish victory at the Battle of Roslin in 1303, alongside John III Comyn, Lord of Badenoch (also known as "Red Comyn"). Here Fraser killed Ralph Manton, an English treasury clerk, whom Fraser accused of embezzling King Edward of funds and neglecting to pay Fraser's wages when he was in English service. Consequently, the English King Edward I, marched north through Stirling taking Perth. As Edward approached Dunfermline, the Bishop of St Andrews and the bishop of Glasgow along with Red Comyn met his army and submitted. Fraser refused to swear fealty to the English King and did not attend.

In March 1304, Fraser and Wallace were ambushed by English forces at the Action at Happrew and defeated. By June Fraser had deserted Wallace and accepted Edward's peace terms. In January 1305 he was employed, along with all other Scottish knights, to hunt down his former comrade Wallace.

In March 1306 Fraser once again broke faith with King Edward and defected to Robert the Bruce. He escaped from the defeat at the Battle of Methven, but was captured during the summer of 1306 at a subsequent engagement at Kirkencliff near Stirling by Sir Thomas de Multon and Sir John Jose. King Edward had commanded all captured supporters of King Robert executed and, in particular, the lands of Simon Fraser harried and burnt. The prisoner was sent to London, and hanged, drawn, and quartered in September 1306. His head was impaled on a spike on London Bridge, along with Wallace's.

«b»Family
Simon is known to have had the following issue:«/b»
1.) Margaret Fraser, married Sir Gilbert Haya of Lochorwart, passing the Barony of Neidpath into the Hay family; had issue.

2.) Joan Fraser, married Sir Patrick Fleming of Biggar, passing the Barony of Oliver into the Fleming family; had issue.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Simon Fraser Filius於大約1266年出生在蘇格蘭皮布萊爾時，他的父親Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle41歲，母親Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle26歲。他和Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness至少有1個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1306年9月8日在英國英格蘭倫敦享年41歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Simon Fraser Filius于大约1266年出生在苏格兰皮布萊爾时，他的父亲Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle41岁，母亲Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle26岁。他和Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness至少有1个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1306年9月8日在英国英格兰倫敦享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fraser
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: apparently a nickname from Anglo-Norman French fraser(e), fresere ‘strawberry plant’. Fresel and Frisel, from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, are early variants of the Fraser surname, the modern Gaelicized form of which is Friseal. See Frizzell . The crest on the Scottish family's coat of arms takes the form of a strawberry plant, but its antiquity is unknown. Claims of a habitational derivation, in particular from a place called la Frézelière in Anjou (France), are attractive but they lack verifiable evidence.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

History: There are two clans Fraser in Scotland, with common ancestry going back to Simon Fraser of Keith in East Lothian, who lived in the 12th century. One of these clans has its seat at Philorth Castle (subsequently re-named Cairnbulg) on the northeast coast of Scotland. Sir Alexander Fraser, 8th laird of Philorth (c. 1536–1623) converted the fishing village of Faithlie into the burgh of Fraserburgh in the 1590s. The other clan is Fraser of Lovat, associated mainly with the city of Inverness are. They are descended from Simon Fraser, a younger son, who lived in the early 14th century. In Gaelic, the head of Clan Fraser of Lovat is known as Mac Shimi ‘son of Simon’.

variants.name: Frayser
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frasure
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frazer
meanings: Variant of   Fraser  , associated chiefly with northern Ireland.

variants.name: Fraizer
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frashier
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .

variants.name: Fraiser
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Fraser  .

variants.name: Frizzell
meanings: English and Irish: nickname from Old French fresel, frisel ‘lace, ribbon, braid, brooch, frill, ruff’, for someone with an ornate style of dress, or ‘object of little value’ (a diminutive of frese, of ancient Germanic origin).
Scottish: from Anglo-Norman French fresel ‘strawberry’, an alternative form of Fraser .

variants.name: Frazier
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Fraser  .
Americanized form of French Frégeau (see Fregeau ).

variants.name: Frasher
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Fraser  .
Possibly an altered form of German Fratzscher (see Frutchey ).

nameMeanings.name: Filius
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German and Dutch: from a short form of the Latin personal name Aemilius (see Milian ).

variants.name: Milian
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Alberto, Francisco, Luis, Raul, Carlos, Mario, Pedro, Ramon, Alfredo, Ana.

Spanish (Milián) and Polish; Czech and Slovak (also Milián): from a vernacular shortened form of the Latin personal name Aemilianus, a derivative of Aemilius, a Roman surname probably derived from aemulus ‘rival’. The name Aemilianus was borne by various early Christian saints (see Millan 1) and hence was widely used throughout Europe as a personal name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Simon Fraser Filius was born about 1266, in Peeblesshire, Scotland, his father, Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle, was 41 and his mother, Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, was 26. He had at least 1 son and 2 daughters with Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. He died on 8 September 1306, in London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Simon Fraser Filius est né environ 1266, à Valle de Twede, Écosse, son père, Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles avec Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1306, à Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Simon Fraser Filius um 1266 in Peeblesshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle 41 und seine Mutter Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle 26. Er hatte mit Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 8. September 1306 im Alter von 41 Jahren in London, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Simon Fraser Filius è nato il circa 1266 nel Peeblesshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie con Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. Morì il 8 settembre 1306, nel London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Simon Fraser Filiusが約1266年にスコットランドペエブレシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castleは41歳，母Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castleは26歳であった。彼はMargaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithnessとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1306年9月8日にイギリスイギリスロンドンで41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Simon Fraser Filius(이)가 대략 1266년 스컷랜드 피블레실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1306년 9월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 런던에서 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Simon Fraser Filius nasceu aproximadamente 1266, em Peeblesshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas com Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1306, em London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Simon Fraser Filius родился около 1266 г. в местности пиблессхир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, 26. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери от Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. Он умер 8 сентября 1306 г. в местности Лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Simon Fraser Filius nació aproximadamente 1266, en Peeblesshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir Simon III Fraser, The Patriot Laird of Oliver Castle and Neidpath Castle, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Lady Mary Bisset of Oliver Castle, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas con Margaret Jarl Jónsdottir of Orkney - one of the heiresses of the earldom of Caithness. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1306, en London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 41 años.
